title: DASATINIB
description: Dasatinib is a potent second-generation tyrosine kinase inhibitor that targets BCR-ABL and Src family kinases. While primarily used as an antineoplastic agent for Philadelphia chromosome-positive leukemias, it is increasingly studied in longevity research as a senolytic due to its ability to selectively induce apoptosis in senescent cells.

research.title: Dasatinib and Quercetin as Senolytic Drugs Improve Fat Deposition and Exhibit Antifibrotic Effects in the Medaka Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatotic Liver Disease Model
research.summary: This study demonstrates that the combination of dasatinib and quercetin effectively removes senescent cells, reduces liver fat deposition, and provides anti-fibrotic benefits.

research.title: Emerging use of senolytics and senomorphics against aging and chronic diseases
research.summary: A review of how dasatinib acts as a senolytic to eliminate cells in cycle arrest, potentially treating age-related disorders like atherosclerosis and type 2 diabetes.

research.title: Real‐World Outcomes With Low‐Dose Dasatinib (50 mg) in Imatinib-Resistant Chronic Myeloid Leukemia
research.summary: Research indicates that a lower dose of 50mg may maintain clinical efficacy while significantly improving the safety profile compared to the standard 100mg dose.

research.title: Drugs induced pulmonary arterial hypertension
research.summary: This paper identifies dasatinib as a known risk factor for developing severe pulmonary arterial hypertension, which is often reversible upon cessation of the drug.

research.title: Effects of Apigenin on Pharmacokinetics of Dasatinib and Probable Interaction Mechanism
research.summary: Study findings suggest that common flavonoids like apigenin can significantly alter the plasma concentration and bioavailability of dasatinib through metabolic interactions.

sentiment.summary: Users are highly optimistic about its powerful senolytic 'age-reversal' potential, but the community is deeply cautious due to its profile as a high-potency chemotherapy drug with serious systemic risks.

effects.label: Synergistic Action:
effects.description: Most effective as a longevity tool when combined with Quercetin (the D+Q protocol) to broaden the range of targeted senescent cells.

effects.label: Intermittent Efficacy:
effects.description: Unlike daily supplements, it is frequently used in 'pulses' to clear cells without constant systemic exposure.

effects.label: Disease Management:
effects.description: Highly effective for imatinib-resistant leukemia, showing deep molecular responses.

dosage.label: Senolytic Protocol:
dosage.description: Common off-label longevity dosage is 100mg taken once weekly or for 2-3 consecutive days once a month.

dosage.label: Standard Clinical Dose:
dosage.description: For leukemia, the approved dose is 100mg daily, though 50mg is increasingly studied for better tolerance.

dosage.label: Pulse Frequency:
dosage.description: Longevity enthusiasts often debate between monthly vs. quarterly cycles to allow for healthy cell recovery.

sideEffects.label: Fluid Retention:
sideEffects.description: Risk of pleural effusion (fluid around the lungs) and peripheral edema even at lower doses.

sideEffects.label: Hematologic Stress:
sideEffects.description: Potential for myelosuppression, including temporary drops in white blood cell and platelet counts.

sideEffects.label: Pulmonary Hypertension:
sideEffects.description: A rare but serious risk of increased blood pressure in the lung arteries.

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Distress:
sideEffects.description: Nausea and hemorrhagic colitis have been reported in clinical settings.

availability.label: Prescription Only:
availability.description: Heavily regulated as a chemotherapy agent, requiring strict medical supervision.

availability.label: High Cost:
availability.description: Traditional pharmaceutical versions are extremely expensive, leading longevity users to seek specialized sourcing or clinical trials.
